frame_analyses.description: First instrument becomes visible and the needle driver/grasper pair begin orienting toward the Penrose target.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Start is clean but there is a brief setup period before the first bite is driven.

frame_analyses.description: Needle being driven through the first side of the Penrose near the marked target.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle angle acceptable; setup and entry somewhat deliberate.

frame_analyses.description: Second bite completed and suture is being pulled through both marks.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good tissue control; some extra instrument travel while drawing the suture through.

frame_analyses.description: Both limbs organized and the long limb is being wrapped to set up the first throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First throw prepared as a surgeon's knot but loops are fairly large and cost time.

frame_analyses.description: First throw passed and seated down onto the Penrose.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot lays down without obvious tearing or avulsion; cinching is controlled.

frame_analyses.description: Reverse single throw being formed and tightened with the opposite hand leading.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Apparent hand switch is correct; motion controlled but not especially compact.

frame_analyses.description: Third throw started with the right-sided instrument leading and loop being passed.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand alternation appears to switch back appropriately; loop size remains somewhat large.

frame_analyses.description: Final throw cinched and knot stack stabilizes over the slit.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final knot appears snug with no obvious loosening during tightening.

frame_analyses.description: Scissors applied to trim the suture tails close to the completed knot.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Cutting performed inside the trainer as required.

frame_analyses.description: Both tails are cut and instruments withdraw, ending the task.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task completed without visible avulsion or knot disruption.

knot_assessments.notes: Right-led double wrap around the opposing instrument before passage and seating.
Single reverse throw formed with left instrument leading, seated in the opposite direction.
Third throw switches back to right-led and remains stable through final tightening.

technique_summary: V12 scored at 114.0s / 483.0 FLS. Successful completion with steady instrument control, no drain avulsion, and a final knot that appears snug without a visible slit gap. Trainee was deliberate rather than fast, especially during limb organization and loop formation, but the required bite placement, three-throw sequence, and intrabox cutting were all completed. Knot-tying phase consumed the majority of the run; cutting was efficient at 4.5s once the knot was finalized.
improvement_suggestions: Load and present the needle in a more ready-to-drive orientation before the first bite to reduce early setup time.
After the second bite, organize the long and short limbs sooner so the transition into the first throw is faster.
Make smaller, tighter loops during the throws to improve economy of motion and shorten knot-tying time.
strengths: All required task elements completed without visible drain avulsion, knot failure, or missed intrabox cuts.
Throw sequence appears correct with hand alternation and a stable final knot over the slit.
comparison_notes: Paired with score_claude_V12_001 — see memory/comparisons/V12_teacher_comparison_20260407.json. ΔFLS 3.5 (strong agreement). Completion time agreement within 1s (114 vs 115). Both teachers agree drain is closed — first V-series video with no drain-gap divergence.
